title: quetzalia occidentalis
intro: Quetzalia occidentalis, also known as the Ocote tree, is a species of tree in the family Burseraceae that is native to Central America and Mexico. This tree has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its resin, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Quetzalia occidentalis include its ability to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The resin can be obtained through various herbal preparations, such as steam distillation, and is often used in traditional medicine to create teas, infusions, and topical applications.
